fields.description:
## Why
An LLM reviewer can invent scale or inflate a small defect via a grand subject -- confident, blocking-eligible FALSE positives. These anchors constrain the reviewers' OWN reasoning (calibration, not detection).

## What (verified edit locations)
- G-9: small-scale default + prohibited-reasoning examples + two-directional bar in plan_review_A1.md and plan_review_T5a.md.
- FP-3(b): make the "blast_radius only LOWERS, never raises" ratchet EXPLICIT + uniform in plan_review_verifier.md + plan_review_verifier_agentic.md prose AND the SeverityAttrs.blast_radius doc in review_kernel/verify.py (the phrase is currently absent from all three).
- Behavioral proof runs on the EVAL-SPEC harness (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml), not mocked unit tests -- calibration of real reviewer reasoning needs the live eval, not a canned response. Add the two scale cases there.
- Back-out: revert the prompt/doc edits (no expand-contract needed) -- note in ticket.

## Provenance
Gap-report labels (G-*/FP-*/R-*/WS-N) are tags from ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020), NOT codebase symbols. Symbols introduced by a SIBLING story (e.g. committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim from WS1) are target-state, not pre-existing; the dependency is recorded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A1/T5a carry the small-scale default + prohibited-reasoning + two-directional text. Proof: grep the phrases in both files.
- [ ] One-way blast_radius phrasing in both verifier prompts + the SeverityAttrs doc. Proof: `grep -l "never raises" src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier*.md src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py`.
- [ ] TWO cases added to plan-review-finder.eval.yaml: a genuine high-scale/high-blast finding still fires; an inflated-scale finding is suppressed. Proof: run the eval-spec suite for those two cases (record both directions), not a mocked unit test.
